---
name: striatum-supervise

# Striatum: Supervised Sessions

RFC 0009 holds a single agent CLI alive across multiple work
packets. Packets arrive as newline-terminated JSON on stdin
(delivered by `striatum supervise send`); the supervised process
reads them line-by-line and reacts through the normal CLI verbs.

## When to invoke

- The lane has `harness_profile.supervision.compatible: true` and
  the operator wants the agent CLI held across packets.
- The operator mentioned `supervise start` / `supervise send`.

## Authoritative reference

- `striatum supervise start` — Start a long-lived agent CLI for the session.
- `striatum supervise send` — Deliver a packet to a supervisor's stdin pipe.
- `striatum supervise stop` — Stop a supervisor (SIGTERM, SIGKILL after 5s).
- `striatum supervise status` — Probe a supervisor's liveness.
- `striatum supervise list` — List supervisors for a run.

Run `striatum <verb> --help` for full flag sets.

## Common patterns

```bash
striatum --repo $REPO supervise start --session-id $SESSION
# packets are delivered via:
striatum --repo $REPO supervise send --supervisor-id $SUP \
    --packet-json '{...}'
striatum --repo $REPO supervise stop --supervisor-id $SUP
```

The supervised process reads packets from stdin one line at a
time. Stdout and stderr are sent to `DEVNULL`; the runner does not
parse supervisor output for state.

## Where to look next

- **`striatum-claim-loop`** — the per-packet verb sequence.
- **`striatum-recover`** — when a supervisor is `lost`.

## What not to do

- Do not write to `.striatum/state.sqlite3` directly; the runner is the only writer (D006/D009).
- Do not treat marker files, tmux panes, or terminal output as workflow state.
- Do not advance state by printing phrases; advance by calling the CLI verbs in your work packet.
- Do not capture stdout/stderr transcripts; D028 keeps them off by default.
- Do not derive bylines from job titles; use the byline supplied in your work packet.
- Do not parse a supervisor's own output for workflow state; supervisors send DEVNULL.
